sections.paragraph:
to desire, to long for, followed by לְ Psa 119:40, 174 Psa 119:174. Of more frequent occurrence in Chaldee. (To this answer אָבָה, אָוָה, and this root may seem to be secondary, and taken from the Hithpael of those verbs.)

Derivative, תַּאֲבָה.

II.

תאב

only found in part. Piel מְתָאֵב i.q. מְתָעֵב abhorring, Amo 6:8 the letters ע and א being interchanged in the Aramean manner, see p. .
